strengths: 15+ years of full-stack engineering experience across diverse domains (AI-agent infrastructure, cloud-native tooling, real-time systems), demonstrating breadth and depth of technical leadership
Proven expertise in AI-related infrastructure (MCP code generators, agent workflows) and open-source contributions (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts), aligning with product/platform strategy focus
Entrepreneurial track record (GISCollective, GISCollective) and startup-like agility in high-impact roles, matching the preferred 'intrapreneurial' profile for cloud/AI platform innovation
concerns: No formal AI/ML degree (PhD/Master's) or explicit AI product ownership on resume, though deep practical expertise exists in adjacent areas
Limited direct experience in 'cloud product strategy' or 'strategic customer advisory' roles, with most roles focused on implementation/engineering
AI expertise is heavily infrastructure- and tooling-oriented (e.g., MCP, agent frameworks) rather than end-user AI product development
response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo’s 15+ years of progressive engineering leadership—spanning AI-agent infrastructure (re:cinq), high-scale systems (Dunnhumby), and open-source platform development (GISCollective)—demonstrate the rare combination of technical depth in AI/cloud tooling and strategic mindset for differentiating products. Her entrepreneurial ventures and intrapreneurial roles (e.g., transforming investment practices at RIDE Capital) further validate her ability to drive innovation in ambiguous, disruptive spaces. While lacking a formal AI degree, her hands-on work with AI frameworks (MCP, agent workflows) and cloud-native systems (Kubernetes, AWS CDK) positions her as a pragmatic builder who can bridge strategy and execution for cloud/AI platform roles.
